PlayHouse Schedule

Radical Queer Playhouse is organized by the BigFatPervs every third Saturday of the month. It is a space where people can come together to find community, share knowledge and experience and learn from others.

Global time schedule

16.30 - 17.00

During this time, you are welcome to come into PlayHouse. If the doors are closed and the curtains are open, please knock and someone inside the space can let you in. During this time, we set up the space together. We move the tables, sweep and mop the floors, make coffee and tea, place mats, and socialize together. We aim to start the PlayHouse at five, but rely on the energy available from participants (including the Big Fat Pervs) to set up the space in time.

17.00

Around this time, we close the doors and curtains to the space and start with an introduction to the space and who we are as participants, facilitators and any other PlayHouse related roles. If you are running late and have communicated with us in advance, we can try to wait for you (max. 15 min). If you arrive late without having communicated with a Playhouse participant and the curtains are closed, please wait until the next timeslot for the doors to be open again. This is after the workshop. There are stores, supermarkets and cafés further down the street if you are looking for a place to wait. The times the doors will be open differ depending on the workshop times. Please check the workshop times from the event you plan to attend to have the most updated information.

17.00 ~ ?

Doors will be closed while workshops are in session in the Main space. We aim to provide a space during and after the workshop where people can socialize and have a practice space. During set-up we decide with the participants present if and how we do this.

20.30 - 21.00

We aim to close all activities at 20:30 and clean up the space together. If during the event everyone has been thoughtful of their participation in a DIY space there won’t be much to do.

The comings and goings during PlayHouse

Due to the themes of the workshops and skill-shares, facilitated by PlayHouse participants, the doors will be closed from outside to create an undisturbed space in which to share. Leaving is of course always permitted. We aim to provide a rest area for people who have need for this. If you would like to know about the resting area, please contact us before the event, or talk to the Care Bears present at PlayHouse.

For the late comers

Participating in a DIY space includes making space for one another and understanding. Also, the public transport in the Netherlands is not punctual, and we know that. If you inform a participant or facilitator about your delay, we can discuss waiting together, others might also be running late for the same reason.

If you would like to collaborate or contribute, please contact us by e-mail bigfatpervs@riseup.net
